questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is not an aerobic activity?
questions.explanation: Sprinting is an anaerobic activity performed in short, intense bursts, whereas swimming, jogging, and cycling are sustained aerobic activities.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following dances is performed by women only?
questions.explanation: Citelele is a traditional Zambian dance performed exclusively by women.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To avoid accidents in a playground, learners must ...
questions.explanation: Obeying instructions/safety rules in a playground is the key way to prevent accidents.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Wearing proper clothing for swimming activities is necessary to ...
questions.explanation: Proper swimwear allows the swimmer to move freely in the water without restriction.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is acceptable in a basketball game?

options.D: Passing the ball with both hands

questions.explanation: Passing the ball with both hands is a legal and acceptable basketball technique; holding, kicking, and pushing are fouls/violations.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
Learners were asked to jump over the vaulting box shown below.
The sport activity they were performing is known as ...
questions.explanation: Jumping over a vaulting box using a springboard is a gymnastic activity.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Reed mats are made by ...
questions.explanation: Reed mats are traditionally made by weaving reed strands together.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Drawing things from the mind is called ... drawing.
questions.explanation: Drawing objects/scenes from the mind without reference is called imaginative drawing.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The music instrument below is ...

options.A: a western musical instrument for western music only.
options.B: a western musical instrument also used with African music.
options.C: an African musical instrument used for African music only.
options.D: an African musical instrument also used with western music.

questions.explanation: The guitar is originally a western instrument but is now widely incorporated and used in African music as well.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following dances are performed at initiation ceremonies?
questions.explanation: Siyemboka and Nkolola are traditional Zambian dances performed specifically during initiation ceremonies.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
Look at the diagram below.
Which point should the shadow of the object be shown?
questions.explanation: A shadow forms on the opposite side of the object from the light source (sun); since the sun's rays point from one side, the shadow falls at point 3 on the opposite side.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mulenga threw a javelin during the inter school sports competition as shown below. His throw was not accepted by the judge. This was because ...

options.B: he stepped beyond the throw mark line.
options.C: his throw went beyond the finishing line.
options.D: his throw did not go very far.

questions.explanation: In javelin throwing, a throw is disqualified if the athlete steps over (beyond) the designated throw line/foul line.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Most Zambian songs are call and response songs. This means they are sung by...

options.A: choir members singing and clapping at the same time.
options.B: elders in a community singing and dancing.
options.C: one person starting a song and others completing a line.
options.D: people singing the same line repetitively.

questions.explanation: Call and response singing involves one person (the caller) starting a phrase, and others responding by completing or answering the line.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which poster is good for a road safety message?

options.C: 3 - Stop check for moving cars cross

questions.explanation: Crossing at the Zebra crossing is the correct, safe road-crossing practice and the most appropriate road safety message.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The teacher told pupils to prepare a variety of dances and songs to be performed during agriculture show celebration. Which one of the following songs was appropriate for the occasion? A song about ...
questions.explanation: A song about poultry (farming/livestock) is directly relevant and appropriate for an agriculture show celebration.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Tourists like buying carved items which are ...
questions.explanation: Tourists are attracted to carved items that are unique, as these stand out and have special appeal as souvenirs.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following places cannot have art works sold quickly?
questions.explanation: A backyard space is a private, low-traffic location, unlike curio shops, exhibition centres, and galleries which are designed for displaying and selling art quickly to the public.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
A pupil was told to do the activity shown below.
This is an example of ...
questions.explanation: Moving through a sequence of different exercise stations (sit ups, squats, push ups, etc.) in a continuous loop is the definition of circuit training.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is not a benefit of swimming activities?

options.D: It is a good skill for catching fish

questions.explanation: Catching fish is not a direct physical/health benefit of swimming; fun, health, and muscle development are the recognised benefits.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mrs Kabale always prepares a list of items and their prices she wants to order from wholesalers. This helps her to know the ...
questions.explanation: A list of items and prices to be ordered from wholesalers relates only to what will be spent, i.e. the expenditure.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Suitable crops for a kitchen garden include:
questions.explanation: Bondwe, cabbage, eggplant, and rape are all common vegetables suited for a small-scale kitchen garden, unlike fruit trees (lemons, oranges, mango) or field crops (maize, groundnuts) which need more space.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Over polished floors and banana peels on the floor are common ...

options.A: causes of accidents in the home.
options.B: signs of cleanliness in the home.
options.D: ways of showing that there is food in the kitchen.

questions.explanation: Over-polished floors and banana peels are slippery and create slip/fall hazards, making them common causes of accidents in the home.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What should be done to get good ventilation?

options.B: Close all the windows during the day time.
options.C: Keep braziers of fire in the house all day.
options.D: Sleep with flowers in the same room

questions.explanation: Avoiding overcrowding in a room allows air to circulate freely, improving ventilation.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The reason to leave openings in clothes is to ...
questions.explanation: Openings (such as for buttons or zippers) in clothes are left primarily to allow the garment to be worn and removed easily.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.topic: Home Economics - Health and Human Development
questions.question: Happiness, fear, anger, surprise are ways of ... expressions.
questions.explanation: Happiness, fear, anger, and surprise are emotions typically displayed through facial expressions.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Kwashiorkor is caused by lack of ... in the body.
questions.explanation: Kwashiorkor is a form of severe malnutrition caused by protein deficiency in the diet.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.topic: Home Economics - Health and Human Development
questions.question: ...% of water makes up the body weight.
questions.explanation: Water makes up approximately 65% (commonly cited as roughly 60-70%) of total human body weight.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Give two reasons why the armpits should be washed properly ...

options.A: avoid bad smell and stains that discolour clothes.
options.B: avoid sweat a lot and keep dry.
options.C: smell good always in clean clothes.
options.D: to be happy and look attractive.

questions.explanation: Washing the armpits properly helps avoid bad body odour and prevents sweat stains that discolour clothing — the two key reasons.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.topic: Home Economics - Health and Human Development
questions.question: What does human development refer to?
questions.explanation: Human development refers to the physical, mental, and social growth of a person over time.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: How many rounded/heaped table spoons of flour make 100 grams?
questions.explanation: Approximately three rounded/heaped table spoons of flour are equivalent to 100 grams, based on standard handy-measure conversion tables used in home economics.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: List the equipment found in a traditional kitchen.
questions.explanation: Small cupboards, a fire place, and a stool are typical equipment/furniture found in a traditional kitchen setting, unlike modern stoves or sinks which are modern additions.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following defines safety in a home?

options.A: Have a first aid kit in the home.
options.B: Keep sharp items where they are easily seen.
options.C: Put medicines on top of cupboards.
options.D: Take medicines whenever you feel sick.

questions.explanation: Having a first aid kit readily available in the home is a key safety practice for responding to injuries.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Where scales are not available, give one example of handy measures that can be used instead.
questions.explanation: A measuring cup is a standard handy-measure tool used in place of scales to estimate quantities of ingredients.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The terms level, rounded and heaped are often used in ... measures.
questions.explanation: The terms level, rounded, and heaped describe how much of an ingredient is on a spoon, so these terms are used in spoon measures.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: ... are known to keep off bad smells in the pin-latrine.
questions.explanation: Ashes are traditionally used in pit-latrines to absorb moisture and keep off bad smells.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Explain why ingredients are weighed. To ...

options.B: discover the exact amount of something.
options.D: find out how heavy something is.

questions.explanation: Ingredients are weighed primarily to ensure accurate proportions, which leads to good/successful cooking results.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The important reason for weighing and measuring ingredients is to ...

options.D: make sure all ingredients are available.

questions.explanation: The overarching purpose of accurately weighing and measuring ingredients is to achieve good results in the final cooked/baked product.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Give a reason why a measuring stone is put on a balance scale.

options.A: For ingredients to rest on a panel.
options.B: For the scale to work properly.
options.D: To know the weight of a stone and ingredients.

questions.explanation: A measuring/calibration stone of known weight is used on a balance scale to ensure the scale gives an accurate weight reading.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Describe the last two procedures for making a toy.
questions.explanation: In making a stuffed toy, the final steps are to put in the stuffing material and then close the opening to complete the toy.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Keeping of records in business will help one to know ...

options.A: losses only in his or her business.
options.B: profits only that he or she is making.
options.C: where to get goods for business.
options.D: whether there is progress or not in the business.

questions.explanation: Keeping business records allows the owner to track overall performance and determine whether the business is progressing (profit or loss), not just one aspect.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is not a text format?
questions.explanation: Bold, Italic, and Normal are standard text formatting styles; 'Round' is not a recognised text format.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Why do people prefer phone calls to letter writing? Phone calls are ...
questions.explanation: Phone calls deliver messages instantly, making them faster than letter writing, which requires time for writing, posting, and delivery.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What do we call drawings done by a builder before building a house?
questions.explanation: Drawings made by a builder before constructing a house are referred to as the plan.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.explanation: In a standard formal/job application letter, the sender's address is conventionally aligned at the top right of the page.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: An example of a source of light in rural areas is ...
questions.explanation: A paraffin lamp is a common, traditional source of light used in rural areas without access to electricity.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What is cursor status? It is ...

options.A: the colour of the cursor when typing.
options.B: the shape and position of the cursor when typing.
options.C: when the cursor is not used during typing.

questions.explanation: Cursor status refers to the shape and position of the cursor as it appears during typing on a computer.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: One of the pupils in grade 5 A was asked by his teacher to add words to the sign shown below. The words she added were ...
questions.explanation: The triangular warning sign showing people crossing represents a pedestrian crossing warning, so the appropriate caption is 'pedestrian crossing'.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Chanda designed the poster below. What type of lettering did he use?
questions.explanation: The poster text 'MAKE SAFE LOVE AIDS KILLS' is written entirely in capital letters, which is upper case lettering.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The sign below was found on the side of the road. Which of the following words can be added to it to make it more meaningful?

options.C: The road has got lines on it.

questions.explanation: The triangular warning sign with a railway gate/crossing symbol warns drivers that there is a railway crossing ahead.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mwanga drew the following objects. Which one of them represents an isometric drawing?

options.C: Larger 3D cube drawing with visible depth

questions.explanation: An isometric drawing shows a three-dimensional object with depth and equal angles between axes; the larger 3D cube drawing with visible depth best represents this.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A pupil drew the symbol shown below and stuck it on her bag. It means ... this bag.
questions.explanation: A hand symbol crossed out with an 'X' is a universal warning symbol meaning 'do not touch'.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: After being typed, a document was being checked for possible miss spellings and missing words. The document was being ...
questions.explanation: Checking a typed document for spelling errors and missing words is the process of editing.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To prevent hand tools from breaking in the workshop, Chawa put them in ...
questions.explanation: Storing hand tools properly in a tool box protects them from damage and breakage.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To construct a radius of a circle below, the following points should be joined together by a straight line.
questions.explanation: A radius is a straight line joining the centre of a circle to a point on its circumference; point B is the centre and point A is on the circumference, so joining A and B forms a radius.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To turn current on and off a ... is used.
questions.explanation: A switch is the device used to turn an electric current on and off.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Someone who discloses his/her ATM number or pin may...

options.A: have his/her money stolen from his/her account.
options.B: have his/her ATM card destroyed or stolen by the bank.
options.C: lose his/her code and the ATM card.

questions.explanation: Disclosing an ATM PIN/number to others creates a security risk, as it can allow unauthorised persons to access and steal money from the account.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following tools is correctly paired with its use?
questions.explanation: A saw is correctly used for cutting wood; the other pairings (chisel-smoothening, hammer-holding, measuring tape-driving) are incorrect uses for those tools.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The diagram below shows a teacher of woodwork demonstrating how to make a ... joint.
questions.explanation: The diagram shows a slot (mortice) being chiselled into one piece of wood to receive a matching peg (tenon) from another piece, which is the mortice and tenon joint.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The letter P can be constructed under ... square boxes.
questions.explanation: The block letter 'P' is conventionally constructed within a 5×4 grid of square boxes in basic lettering/graphic design exercises.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Traditional dance is important in the history and culture of Zambia because it ...

options.A: helps to separate families in society.
options.B: places men to be superior over women folk.
options.C: passes on belief and history to future generations.

questions.explanation: Traditional dance serves as a way of passing on cultural beliefs and history from one generation to the next.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Why are swimmers asked to have a shower with soap before they can swim in a pool? It ...

options.A: helps to keep the swimming pool clean.
options.B: helps swimmers to be more flexible.

questions.explanation: Showering with soap before swimming removes dirt, sweat, and oils from the body, helping to keep the pool water clean.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a traditional basic equipment?
questions.explanation: A brazier is a traditional cooking/heating equipment used over charcoal, unlike the cooker, colander, and sink which are modern kitchen equipment.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Simutala sales groceries in a shop. Every day he records all his sales and items. He does this in order to know ...

options.B: how to be nice to customers.
options.D: whether he makes profit or loss.

questions.explanation: Recording daily sales and items helps a business owner track finances and determine whether the business is making a profit or a loss.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mabvuto wanted to stretch a cold muscle but was stopped by his teacher. This was because ...

options.A: the muscle was going to become weak.
options.B: he was going to injure himself.
options.C: he could not maintain its shape.
options.D: he could not maintain its strength.

questions.explanation: Stretching a cold (unwarmed) muscle can cause injury such as strains or tears, which is why warming up is required before stretching.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.explanation: Dribbling (running while bouncing the ball) is a defining feature of basketball.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Shila performed a dance where she could follow specific steps and moves. This dance is called ... movement.
questions.explanation: A dance following specific, pre-set steps and moves is known as structured movement, as opposed to freestyle (improvised) movement.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: In which swimming race are participants allowed to use all the types of strokes?
questions.explanation: Freestyle swimming allows the swimmer to use any stroke of their choice, unlike backstroke and breaststroke which require a specific technique.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The makishi dance is a cultural ceremony that is performed by ... speaking people.
questions.explanation: The Makishi masked dance ceremony is traditionally performed by the Luvale-speaking people of Zambia during initiation rites.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The efficiency of the heart, the blood vessels and the lungs is called ...
questions.explanation: Cardiovascular fitness refers to how efficiently the heart, blood vessels, and lungs work together to supply oxygen to the body.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.explanation: Dressing nicely and decently gives a person a respectable appearance.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.explanation: Colours positioned directly opposite each other on the colour wheel are called complementary colours.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To compose African music one should start with a ...
questions.explanation: In composing African music, the process typically begins with establishing a theme, followed by a tune, and then choreographing a dance to match.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: A picture made from collection of different pictures that are fitted together to make one picture is called ...
questions.explanation: A montage is created by combining multiple separate pictures/images together to form a single composite picture.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Enala mixed blue and green colours during a practical lesson. What type of colour did she make?
questions.explanation: Mixing a primary colour (blue) with an adjacent secondary colour (green) produces an intermediate (tertiary) colour; here it is classified as an intermediate colour.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The five lines on which music is written are called ...
questions.explanation: The five horizontal lines on which musical notes are written are called the stave (or staff).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Jane made it difficult for the opponent player in a volleyball game to return the ball by ...
questions.explanation: An overhead serve is hit with more power and speed, making it harder for the opponent to return the ball.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: In which of the gymnastic styles below is energy produced without oxygen being supplied to the muscles?
questions.explanation: Anaerobic means 'without oxygen'; anaerobic exercise produces energy without oxygen being supplied to the muscles.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To produce good and strong clay bricks, John was advised to use clay from ...
questions.explanation: Clay found underground (subsoil) is denser and free of organic matter/roots, making it suitable for producing strong bricks.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Jane wants to take body measurements so that she can sew a dress. Which of the tools below is she going to use?
questions.explanation: A flexible tape measure (item iv) is the correct tool for taking body measurements, as it can bend around body curves unlike a rigid ruler.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What is pool hygiene?

options.C: Making sure the water is flowing.
options.D: Painting the wall of the swimming pool blue.

questions.explanation: Pool hygiene refers to keeping the swimming pool clean and clear of dirt and contamination for safe use.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Applying safety rules help people ...
questions.explanation: Safety rules exist specifically to help prevent accidents and injuries.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Why should finger nails be kept short? To ...
questions.explanation: Keeping fingernails short helps prevent dirt and germs from accumulating underneath them, making it easier to keep them clean.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Describe one way to have good ventilation at night.

options.B: Do not over crowd in the room
options.D: Sleep with flowers in the room

questions.explanation: Avoiding overcrowding in a room at night allows for better air circulation and ventilation.

questions.topic: Home Economics - Health and Human Development
questions.question: Which of these statements describe the function of the skin?

options.B: Expose the body to air borne diseases.

questions.explanation: One of the main functions of the skin is to act as a protective barrier against germs and infections.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: What else do we use to unblock the kitchen sink apart from the suction pump?
questions.explanation: Hot water can help dissolve grease and clear blockages in a kitchen sink, serving as an alternative to a suction pump.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.explanation: Cups and spoons are standard measuring tools used in cooking for both volume (cups) and small measures (spoons).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.explanation: Hard boiled egg, cucumber, and tomato are commonly used as fresh, suitable garnishes for a cabbage/vegetable salad.

options.D: money given to an injured persons relatives.

options.B: prevent him from seeing the pin number of the other customer.
options.C: prevent the ATM from retaining the card.
options.D: protect the ATM from being damaged.

questions.explanation: Standing back at an ATM protects another customer's privacy by preventing onlookers from seeing their PIN number, which helps prevent fraud.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies

questions.explanation: Posters are effective communication tools because they can visually depict different situations and messages clearly to an audience.

questions.question: The method of water purification shown in the diagram is called ...
questions.explanation: The diagram shows dirty water passing through layers of stones, sand, and cloth to produce clean water, which is the process of filtration.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following activities cannot be turned into a business venture in Home Economics?
questions.explanation: Plumbing is a Technology Studies/technical trade skill, not a Home Economics activity, unlike flower arrangement, gardening, and scone baking.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Crocheting can be used for ... on garments.
questions.explanation: Crocheting is commonly used to add decorative trims and patterns to garments.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following water treatments is correctly paired with its explanation?

options.C: Decantation - Pouring out water from a settled mixture
options.D: Filtration - Straining dirty water through muslin cloth

questions.explanation: Filtration correctly refers to straining dirty water through a cloth (such as muslin) to remove impurities; the other pairings describe the wrong process for each term.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The two sources of proteins are ...
questions.explanation: Protein comes from two main sources: animal sources (meat, eggs, dairy) and plant sources (beans, legumes).

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the pieces shown below can be joined when making a shirt?
questions.explanation: The sleeve (piece 2) is joined to the front shirt body (piece 4) at the armhole when constructing a shirt.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: ... is a common type of weave used in needlework and crafts.
questions.explanation: Plain weave is the simplest and most common type of weave used in basic needlework and crafts.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.topic: Home Economics - Health and Human Development
questions.question: Which of the following is not an effect of physical appearance on others?
questions.explanation: Intelligence is an intellectual trait, not a visible effect produced by physical appearance, unlike attraction, repulsion, or respect which can be influenced by how someone looks.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: John's money was stolen from his bank account. He might have ...

options.A: disclosed his ATM card PIN to someone.
options.C: too much money in his account.
options.D: too little money in his account.

questions.explanation: Disclosing an ATM PIN to someone else is a common way money gets stolen from a bank account.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The key marked P on the calculator drawn below is meant to ...
questions.explanation: Key P points to the 'C' (Clear) key area; however, in context with the calculator layout shown, this key is associated with finding/clearing results before a new calculation, matching option C.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the labelled parts show a switch on the diagram below?
questions.explanation: Part A in the circuit diagram shows the break in the wire with a movable arm, which is the standard symbol for a switch.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: ... are a product of moulding.
questions.explanation: Bricks are produced through moulding clay or cement into shape, unlike brooms, chairs, or mats which are made by other methods.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following polygon is correctly paired with its name?
questions.explanation: Option A correctly pairs a five-sided shape with the name 'pentagon'; the other options mismatch the shape with an incorrect name.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Edward opened a barbershop in town after being empowered by the government. He offered ... to the people.
questions.explanation: A barbershop offers haircuts and grooming, which are services, not physical goods.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which type of a calculator is designed to solve problems in science, mathematics and engineering?
questions.explanation: A scientific calculator is specifically designed with functions for solving science, mathematics, and engineering problems.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the shapes below has a diameter, radius and centre?
questions.explanation: Only a circle has a diameter, radius, and centre as defining geometric properties.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Mr Jones bought a car at K35 000 and later sold it at K45 000. How much profit did he make?
questions.explanation: Profit = Selling Price − Cost Price = K45 000 − K35 000 = K10 000.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The following are components of a simple electric circuit except ...
questions.explanation: Rubber is an insulator and does not conduct electricity, so it is not a component of a simple electric circuit, unlike bulb, copper wire, and switch.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: ... is used by professional graphic artists and photographers to create new images.
questions.explanation: Photoshop is a well-known professional software used by graphic artists and photographers to create and edit images.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Tom was asked to construct a radius of a circle below. Which points would he join?
questions.explanation: A radius joins the centre of the circle to a point on its circumference; point B is the centre and point C is on the circumference, so the radius joins B and C.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Choose the correct procedure to be followed when gluing materials.

options.A: (i) Apply glue on both parts. (ii) Clean the surfaces to be joined. (iii) Clamp the two pieces of wood together.
options.B: (i) Clamp the two pieces of wood together. (ii) Clean the surfaces to be joined. (iii) Apply glue on both parts.
options.C: (i) Clean the surfaces to be joined. (ii) Apply glue on both parts. (iii) Clamp the two pieces of wood together.
options.D: (i) Clean the surfaces to be joined. (ii) Clamp the two pieces of wood together. (iii) Apply glue on both parts.

questions.explanation: The correct gluing procedure is to first clean the surfaces, then apply glue, and finally clamp the pieces together while the glue sets.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
A learner constructed the block figure below.
It is drawn in ...
questions.explanation: The 3D L-shaped block drawn with parallel edges at equal angles (typical isometric style) represents an isometric drawing.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Moving a cursor with ... is easier and quicker than using a mouse.
questions.explanation: Arrow keys on a keyboard can move the cursor directly and are often quicker for short-distance movement than a mouse.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Which of the following text format slants the letters evenly to the right?
questions.explanation: Italic formatting slants letters evenly to the right.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: To prevent twisting and cracking of bricks, Jane made a simple shade. This type of drying bricks is known as ...
questions.explanation: Drying bricks under a simple constructed shade (rather than direct sunlight) is a controlled, artificial drying method used to prevent twisting and cracking.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: The nails in the diagram below are used to fix ...
questions.explanation: The bent-head roofing-style nails shown are typically used to fix ordinary iron roofing sheets onto wooden roof structures.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question:
Agness wanted to access different computer applications. What did she use?
Microsoft ...
questions.explanation: Microsoft Office is a suite of applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.), so accessing 'different computer applications' implies using Microsoft Office.

questions.subject: Creative and Technology Studies
questions.question: Changing the size of an image is a type of graphics known as ...
questions.explanation: Resizing an image is a form of graphic manipulation, which involves altering an image's properties such as size, colour, or shape.
